name: Regional Finance
description_short: Regional Finance offers personal loans up to $50K for debt consolidation, purchases, and expenses at a Houston branch, serving borrowers with less-than-perfect credit.
description_long:
Regional Finance has operated for over 30 years as a personal finance lender, establishing itself as a neighborhood lender focused on serving customers who may not qualify for traditional bank loans. The company positions itself as a financial partner rather than a transactional lender, emphasizing personalized service through local branch relationships. Their Houston location, managed by Shudney Crumby, operates standard business hours (8:30 AM–5:30 PM weekdays) and offers in-person consultations plus appointment-based service outside posted hours.

Regional Finance's service menu includes personal loans, auto-secured loans, debt consolidation loans, appliance financing, furniture loans, auto repair loans, travel loans, holiday loans, and loan insurance. They explicitly market to borrowers with less-than-perfect credit and support various use cases: debt consolidation, home repairs, medical expenses, childcare costs, appliance replacement, vacations, and unexpected financial needs. The prequalification process is designed to be accessible—customers can check eligibility online without a hard credit pull, then proceed to full application either in-branch or by phone.

The company differentiates itself through extended tenure (30+ years in business), flexible payment options, willingness to work with subprime borrowers, and localized branch service with a named manager. Their application process emphasizes transparency: they require standard documentation (government ID, proof of residence, proof of income) and clearly communicate the approval workflow. The website avoids aggressive marketing tactics, instead focusing on education about their process and customer-friendly features like free prequalification.

A significant limitation is the lack of published rate information, terms, or APR ranges on the website—borrowers cannot comparison shop before engaging. The company does not disclose typical loan amounts, repayment periods, or cost structures. The website also provides no independent customer reviews despite claiming "trusted reviews from real customers," suggesting review links may be broken or removed. As a storefront lender requiring in-person or phone interaction for final approval, Regional Finance may not suit borrowers seeking rapid digital-only processes or transparent upfront pricing.
pros: Over 30 years in business, indicating operational stability and customer longevity
Explicitly serves borrowers with less-than-perfect credit, removing stigma from subprime lending
Free prequalification available online without hard credit pull to check eligibility first
Multiple loan products beyond personal loans (auto-repair, furniture, appliances, debt consolidation)
Flexible payment options designed to accommodate different budget situations
Local branch with named manager (Shudney Crumby) available for personalized service
Extended hours and appointment availability beyond posted hours (8:30 AM–5:30 PM Mon–Fri)
cons: No published APR, rate ranges, or loan term details on website—pricing is opaque and requires direct contact
No independent customer reviews displayed despite claiming trusted reviews; review links appear nonfunctional
Requires in-person or phone interaction for application completion; no fully digital/online process disclosed
No information about loan amounts, repayment periods, or typical costs for comparison shopping
Single Houston branch location limits accessibility for customers outside that area
best_for: Borrowers with fair or poor credit seeking personal loans in the Houston area
Customers needing debt consolidation with a local lender willing to work with subprime profiles
People requiring flexible financing for specific purchases (appliances, furniture, auto repairs) and preferring in-person relationship lending
Borrowers who value transparent application processes and named point-of-contact service over fully digital lending
diagnosis: Regional Finance is best suited for Houston-area borrowers with fair to poor credit who need $1K–$50K in personal or specialty financing and prefer working with a local branch and named manager. The primary caveat is complete lack of published pricing, rates, or terms online—borrowers must contact the lender directly to evaluate cost and cannot comparison shop beforehand, making it impossible to assess true competitiveness.
